hypothesis: Fixing the aromatic valency rounding bug and training 3D molecular generative models on chemically accurate (aromatic-dependent or Kekulized) datasets will produce generated molecules with higher chemical validity and significantly smaller geometry deviations and relaxation energies (lower Δr, Δθ, Δφ, and ΔE_relax measured by GFN2-xTB) than models trained on the original flawed dataset.
